groups.name: Stranger Things
groups.explanation: Hawkins, Indiana (location), Eleven (character), Winona Ryder (actor), and The Upside Down (location/concept) are all from Stranger Things.

groups.name: Fictional Towns in Indiana or Ohio-Alike Midwest Comedies — TRAP: these are all fictional TV towns but NOT from the same show
groups.explanation: Greendale (Community), Pawnee (Parks and Recreation), Eagleton (Parks and Recreation — yes, a second P&R town is the trap), and Springfield (The Simpsons) are all fictional towns from different TV shows. The trap: Pawnee AND Eagleton are both from Parks and Rec, making players second-guess, but the group connection is fictional TV towns, not same-show towns.

groups.name: Mad Men universe
groups.explanation: Don Draper (character), Dick Whitman (Don Draper's real name / secret identity), Jon Hamm (actor), and Sterling Cooper (fictional ad agency / location) are all from Mad Men. The trap is that Dick Whitman looks unrelated and Don Draper might seem to belong to an 'antihero' group.

groups.name: Fictional TV towns that are also real U.S. city names
groups.explanation: Albuquerque (Breaking Bad), Dillon (Friday Night Lights), Cicely (Northern Exposure), and Stars Hollow (Gilmore Girls) are all fictional or semi-fictional small-town TV settings that share their names with real American places — and each is the defining small-town or city setting of its respective show. The difficulty comes from Albuquerque (a real major city used as a fictional backdrop) sitting alongside tiny invented towns, and players may wrongly assume Albuquerque belongs with a Breaking Bad group.
